ddls:
CREATE TABLE "public_figures" (
  "figure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public figure */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the public figure */,
  "screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name or handle of the public figure */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography or description of the public figure */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the public figure's profile image */,
  "platforms" TEXT /* Array of social media platforms the public figure is active on */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the public figure follows */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the account is verified */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the public figure */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("figure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "figure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the public figure who posted the tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "link" TEXT /* Link to the tweet */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was posted */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "favorites" INTEGER /* Number of favorites */,
  "mentions" TEXT /* Mentions in the tweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Hashtags in the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., web, mobile app) */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_of_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if it is a retweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_figure_id FOREIGN KEY ("figure_id") REFERENCES public_figures ("figure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time when the user accessed the platform */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mention */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet containing the mention */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the mentioned user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mentions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet containing the hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag text */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who retweeted */,
  "retweet_date" TEXT /* Date and time the retweet was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "favorites" (
  "favorite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each favorite */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who favorited */,
  "favorite_date" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was favorited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("favorite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_favorites_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., 'lang', 'source') */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_metadata_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., 'followers_count', 'friends_count') */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_metadata_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_figure_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "figure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the public figure */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key of the statistic (e.g., 'tweet_count', 'follower_count') */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the statistic */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the statistics were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_figure_statistics_figure_id FOREIGN KEY ("figure_id") REFERENCES public_figures ("figure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date and time of the session */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date and time of the session */,
  "device" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the session was initiated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
